LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM ALBER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE VOID DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS TIMELY. THE DECLARATION OF MR. ALBER SUFFICIENTLY DEMONSTRATES THAT HE WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT BY SUBSTITUTED SERVICE. (SEE DECLARATION OF WILLIAM ALBER IN SUPPORT OF MOTION, 7-8.) THE PRESENT MOTION IS NOT A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION UNDER CCP 1008 BECAUSE THE COURT DID NOT DENY DEFENDANT'S MOTION NOVEMBER 7, 2013; THE MOTION WAS SIMPLY TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS UNTIMELY (CCP 1005(B); 1013(A).) DEFENDANT SHALL FILE ITS PROPOSED ANSWER AND PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITHIN 5 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Renewed Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Void Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WILLIAM ALBER'S Motion To Set Aside Void Default And Default Judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's motion is timely. The Declaration of Mr. Alber sufficiently demonstrates that he was not properly served with the Summons and Complaint by substituted service. (See Declaration of William Alber in Support of Motion, 7-8.) The present motion is not a motion for reconsideration under CCP 1008 because the Court did not deny Defendant's motion November 7, 2013; the motion was simply taken off calendar as untimely (CCP 1005(b); 1013(a).) Defendant shall file its proposed answer and proposed cross-complaint within 5 days after notice of entry of this order. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Discharged

Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-COMPLAINANT WILLIAM ALBER's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT is Off calendar as untimely. (CCP 1005(b), 1013(a).) The motion was mail-served on October 16, 2013. Moving party did not account for the extension of the notice period by 5 calendar days for service by mail. (CCP 1013(a).) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Prove Up Hearing And Summary Of The Case
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Vantree, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Steven D. Cribb, Esq. appeared for plaintif Northern California Collection Service, Inc.
Witness Dixie Rogers was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant William Francis Alber

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc and against the Defaulted Defendant William Francis Alber, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $177,554.91
Prejudgment Interest - $40,225.28
Attorney Fees - $1,000.00
Costs - $575.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $219,355.19
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
